creator: glebius@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Thu, Sep 14, 2006 at 08:46:08AM +0000, Imri Zvik wrote:
I> >Environment:
I> FreeBSD wintergreen 6.1-STABLE FreeBSD 6.1-STABLE #1: Wed Aug 30 09:54:35 IDT 2006     root@wintergreen:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/IGLD  i386
I> >Description:
I> I've recently upgraded my 6.0-STABLE machine to 6.1-STABLE, and since the upgrade i'm getting "em0: watchdog timeout -- resetting" messages every once in a while (sometimes twice a day, sometimes every couple of days). This causes the machine to be unstable, network-wise (connections being dropped, etc.)
I> 
I> Sep 14 06:35:41 wintergreen kernel: em0: watchdog timeout -- resetting
I> Sep 14 06:35:41 wintergreen kernel: em0: link state changed to DOWN
I> Sep 14 06:35:42 wintergreen kernel: em0: link state changed to UP
I> 
I> 
I> i've found this discussion on freebsd-stable (http://www.mail-archive.com/freebsd-stable@freebsd.org/msg81865.html) indicating that i am not the only one suffering from this issue.

As I already have mentioned many times on the mailing lists. Before
revision 1.65.2.14 of if_em.c, the watchdog timeout event's was not
logged at all.

However, there is definitely a regression in RELENG_6 that leads to
fake(?) watchdog timeouts on some systems. Unfortunately, I haven't
yet reproduced it on my systems.

Some people report that this is SMP issue, setting debug.mpsafenet=0
plumbs the problem.

Not sure whether you have the regression, or you are seeing the watchdog
events, that were not logged before.

-- 
Totus tuus, Glebius.
GLEBIUS-RIPN GLEB-RIPE
